# Shane Gring

> Fractional COO for founder-dependent businesses. I take what lives in the founder's head (judgment, methodology, the way work actually gets done) and turn it into operations that don't need them in every meeting.

## About

Shane Gring is a fractional Chief Operating Officer based in the United States. Fifteen-plus years building the operational layer underneath businesses that grew faster than their ops, marketing, or methodology could keep up.

The throughline of his work: businesses know what they do, but the way it gets done lives inside one person: usually the founder, sometimes the expert who started it. Shane gets it out. He maps the judgment, externalizes the methodology, and builds the systems (operations, marketing ops, credentialing, governance) that let other people run the work without losing what made it good in the first place. AI is the current best tool for this work, used as guardrails rather than as a replacement.

Education: Miami University.

## Approach

Shane builds the website as the operating layer a business runs from: not a brochure to be looked at, but the surface the team runs on. The same jobs marketing software promises to automate (pages, SEO, AI search, conversion) done by an operator, then transferred so the site keeps working without the founder. Full point of view: https://shanegring.com/approach.html

## Services: The Modern Operations Path™

The Modern Operations Path™: five steps, each crediting into the next, prices are public, and no step requires a discovery call to learn the price. Overview: https://shanegring.com/work-with-me

1. **[The Scan](https://shanegring.com/scan)** — Free, two minutes. A scored, automated report on what search and AI engines can actually read about a business from its website.
2. **[The Read](https://shanegring.com/read)** — $450, 5 business days. A 30-40 minute recorded walkthrough of the site by Shane plus a findings memo: where the operating logic is missing, what AI engines get wrong about the business, and a ranked effort-versus-impact list of fixes. Credits in full toward the Operating Map within 30 days.
3. **[The Operating Map](https://shanegring.com/operating-map.html)** — $1,999, three 90-minute working sessions over three weeks. Extracts the operating logic of the business into the Operating Memo, with a fixed-price build proposal as the memo's final page. Credits in full toward a build within 60 days. Capped at four Maps a month. Every build starts with a Map, no exceptions.
4. **[The Operating Site](https://shanegring.com/operating-site)** — Fixed price, scoped from the Map (the build proposal is the Operating Memo’s final page). Core build 6-8 weeks, Full build 8-12 weeks. The website replaced with a componentized, machine-readable operating surface: structured data on every page, a content system the client's team can run, documentation as a deliverable, a 90-minute handoff session. First Partner month included with every build.
5. **[The Operating Partner](https://shanegring.com/operating-partner)** — Starts at $3,500/month (Partner+ $6,000/month, available after three months), six-month minimum. A monthly operating rhythm: a 45-minute review, the site updated to match the business, search and AI visibility monitored with fixes shipped rather than listed, and one new asset built every month.

## Guides

Plain-language answers to the questions behind founder-dependent websites, written out in full: https://shanegring.com/guides/

- [Can AI read your website?](https://shanegring.com/guides/can-ai-read-my-website) — what machines actually read, the five signals that decide it, and the ten-minute check.
- [Why your website isn't bringing in clients](https://shanegring.com/guides/why-your-website-isnt-bringing-in-clients) — it's rarely the design; where the real leaks are.
- [The search signals that cost nothing](https://shanegring.com/guides/seo-basics-that-cost-nothing) — one H1, honest titles, real link labels, alt text, a sitemap.
- [Should you block AI crawlers?](https://shanegring.com/guides/should-i-block-ai-crawlers) — the honest trade behind robots.txt and llms.txt.
- [Build a content layer](https://shanegring.com/guides/build-a-content-layer) — turning what you already know into pages that earn cold search.
- [One page per service](https://shanegring.com/guides/one-page-per-service) — why each service needs its own indexed page.
- [Schema for service businesses](https://shanegring.com/guides/schema-for-services) — structured data that tells machines what you sell.
- [Everything runs through you](https://shanegring.com/guides/everything-runs-through-you) — getting the founder's judgment out of their head.
- [A redesign won't fix it](https://shanegring.com/guides/redesign-wont-fix-it) — redesign versus rebuild, and how to tell which you need.
- [Your website was true at launch](https://shanegring.com/guides/your-website-was-true-at-launch) — keeping the site matched to the business as it moves.

## Free tools

- **[Site Readiness Scan](https://shanegring.com/scan)**: Enter a URL and get a scored, sectioned read of how ready a site is for AI: whether AI can read it, whether it can drive its own SEO and content, and whether it could become a surface you build on rather than a brochure you maintain. The free machine version of the Read.

## MCP server

This site is also a Model Context Protocol server. AI assistants can connect at https://mcp.shanegring.com/mcp (streamable HTTP, no login) and call three tools: get_operating_path (the five steps and prices), about_shane (background, work, contact), and run_site_scan (the free Site Readiness Scan, run on any URL). In Claude: Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector.

## Who hires Shane

Businesses where one or more of the following are true:
- Every important decision still routes through the founder.
- The team can ship, but only after the founder reviews it.
- Senior talent has left, or the current leader isn't equipped for the next step.
- Marketing is inconsistent because nobody else can hold the standard.
- Smart hires still wait for the founder's direction.
- The methodology lives in heads and a Google Doc nobody updates.
- New customers are getting more complex than the operating model can handle.
- The founder is doing both the founder job and the COO job.

## Selected work

- **TeamBuildr**: Fractional Marketing & Revenue Ops Consultant. Strength-and-conditioning platform behind 5,500+ organizations (high schools to the Denver Nuggets). Reorganizing the team, building AI into marketing policy and practice, coaching the emerging leadership layer. https://teambuildr.com
- **SEAM**: Fractional COO. The world's first social-impact certification for commercial real estate, GRESB-recognized. Moved SEAM from idea-phase nonprofit into a business: led the rebrand, rebuilt the site on modern AI and marketing tooling, liberated the standard from a 300-page PDF into a lean CMS-managed digital standard. https://seamcertification.com
- **DRVN Golf**: Operations Lead. Scaled DRVN from a consumer golf-fitness app into a network-based ecosystem of Certified Pros, Partner facilities, a creator program, and the Golf Fitness Handicap™ as a measurable standard. https://drvngolf.com
- **Certainly**: Cooperative founder/operator. A cooperative of fractional workers across industries. Members buy in and get projects, insurance, benefits, and a community. Clients get a coordinated bench of fractional talent that can show up as one team. https://certainly.coop
- **International WELL Building Institute (IWBI)**: Technology & Marketing Partner. Ten-year engagement across IWBI's growth from 10 to 130 people. At peak ran a 30-person team inside the relationship: copywriting, design, dev, ads, product, and data. WELL covers 6 billion+ square feet with nearly 30,000 Accredited Professionals. Currently technology and AI consultant to the marketing team. https://wellcertified.com
- **U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC)**: Community Development Manager. Built a national community development program from the ground up: a lightweight path to sustainability for organizations priced out of LEED (nonprofits, communities of faith, YMCAs, affordable housing operators). Engaged hundreds of volunteers, dozens of organizations, and 8.5M+ sq ft of under-resourced real estate. https://usgbc.org

## Notable affiliations & recognition

- Unreasonable Institute Fellow
- Hitachi Foundation Fellow
- LEED AP Homes

## How Shane works

Every engagement starts the same way: getting inside how the business actually runs: the meetings, the judgment calls, the things that happen because the founder is in the room. From there: map the transfer. What becomes a system, what becomes someone else's job, what becomes automation. Then build it.

The work runs on The Modern Operations Path™: the free Scan, then the Read ($450), then the Operating Map ($1,999), then the Operating Site build (fixed price, scoped from the Map), then the Operating Partner retainer (from $3,500/month). Each step credits into the next, so a client who builds paid nothing for the diagnostics; a client who stops anywhere owns everything produced to that point.
